meta_title: For Students: marketing automation to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Marketing Automation to Solve Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how to use marketing automation, Antigravity, and Claude Code to finish your first real-world SaaS app as a student.
content.answer_block: To stop abandoning projects, you must automate the feedback loop. By integrating marketing automation, you turn a 'finished' build into a living entity that generates interest while you code. Use Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code to handle the boilerplate, ensuring you ship functional, market-ready features faster.
content.intro:
Your GitHub profile is a graveyard of half-baked repos and abandoned 'next big things.' You start with high-octane energy, but as the complexity of deployment and user acquisition hits, your motivation evaporates. It is the classic student's trap: falling in love with the code, but failing to build the machine.

But what if your project marketed itself? By treating marketing automation as a core feature rather than an afterthought, you remove the psychological friction of building in a void. This case study shows you how to leverage modern AI tools to ship a project that actually lives in the wild.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Stop building features that don't matter. Focus on one core problem and use AI to scope the smallest viable version.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Given my goal of [Project Name], define the absolute minimum features required to launch. Keep it to 3 core screens and 1 API integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'create a project roadmap for [Goal] focusing only on MVP features'

steps.title: Automate User Onboarding
steps.description: Use automated email sequences to nurture users, so your project has a purpose from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a schema for a user onboarding flow that triggers a welcome email via Resend API when a user registers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'implement a basic Node.js webhook that triggers a welcome email using Resend'

steps.title: Implement Social Proof Hooks
steps.description: Automate the sharing of user milestones to build your project's reputation while you sleep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a function that automatically generates a summary post of a user's recent activity in the app and formats it for X/Twitter.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'add a service layer to post user milestone summaries to Twitter API'

steps.title: Feedback Loop Integration
steps.description: Automate the collection of user feedback to ensure you are building what people actually want.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a feedback form component that sends data to a Notion database automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'connect the feedback form to Notion API using a serverless function'

steps.title: Continuous Deployment Setup
steps.description: Remove the friction of 'shipping' by automating your deployment pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs tests and deploys to Vercel on every main branch push.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'generate a .github/workflows/deploy.yml for Vercel deployment'

pro_tips: Never build without a 'kill switch'—if a feature takes more than 4 hours to code, simplify it.
Use Claude Code to write your README.md first; it forces you to clarify your value proposition before writing a single line of logic.
Automate your analytics early; knowing 10 people used your app is more motivating than guessing if anyone cares.
Treat your marketing automation as a 'user'—if the automated emails are boring to you, they will be boring to your customers.
Focus on the 'Day 1' experience; most students fail because the setup is too hard, not because the code is bad.
content.next_steps: After completing these steps, deploy your MVP to a public URL and share it in our community Discord to get your first 10 real users.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your projects die in the draft folder. Use these tools to build, automate, and ship your first real-world SaaS. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
